mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day SPX returns during tightening periods, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer income, which consistently supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or inflation spikes fail to predict subsequent equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals weakening aggregate demand momentum, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence term shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX direction, or positive divergence yields positive returns instead of negative.

mechanism_narrative: Negative industrial production growth during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply-side constraints that limit corporate output and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during recovery phases fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal reverses direction in this regime.
